bullets.text: Q3 combined ratio was 92.5, consistent with the year-to-date 92.9; all product lines performed within acceptable targets, aided by favorable special lines seasonality and a below-average hurricane season.
Overall written premium grew 6% for the quarter and year-to-date; Direct auto showed strong double-digit monthly written premium growth while Agency auto was described as 'flat-out disappointing.'

Direct auto PIF grew ~7% with new applications also up ~7%; Agency auto new applications deteriorated significantly after a strong start to 2014, reflecting ongoing competitive pricing challenges in the channel.

Agency auto growth was a top priority concern; product design, underwriting, and ease-of-use modifications were underway, with Massachusetts Snapshot design improvements planned for broader rollout starting late 2014.

Auto frequency trends that suggested further rate action earlier in the year had mitigated; personal injury protection severity trends were flagged as an area receiving attention despite overall favorable results.

Three new Service Centers opened, bringing the total to 65; ~25% of all repairable vehicles were being processed through Service Centers nationally, at the lowest LAE levels and highest claim quality measures.

Net income was up 27% for the quarter but only 5% year-to-date due to sluggish investment contributions; management maintained short-duration fixed-income positioning despite frustration with low yields.

Renters insurance product launched in two states during Q3 with additional rollout planned through year-end and into 2015; Commercial Lines refocused on growth from a position of restored profitability.
